_comment: MCP server template — observability platform (logs, metrics, traces). Fill in the provider-specific details for your stack.
name: observability
description: Query logs, metrics, and distributed traces from the platform observability stack

transport.command: <FILL_IN: command to start your observability MCP server, e.g. npx -y @your-org/mcp-server-datadog>

env.OBSERVABILITY_API_KEY: <FILL_IN: API key for your observability platform>
env.OBSERVABILITY_BASE_URL: <FILL_IN: base URL for your platform's API, e.g. https://api.datadoghq.com>

config.defaultService: <FILL_IN: optional — pre-scope queries to a single service>

_notes: Replace the transport.command with the MCP server for your specific observability platform.
Keep API keys scoped to read-only where possible — Claude should be querying, not modifying alert rules.
Popular open-source MCP servers for observability: check the MCP server registry at https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers.
If no MCP server exists for your platform, the internal-api.json template can be adapted to call your platform's REST API directly.
